metadata.title: JRN Multiplicative Compounding: 2x2 Probe Grid on rel-f1
metadata.description: Self-contained experiment measuring individual JRN for 13 FK joins using MLP and GBM probes, chain JRN for 12 multi-hop chains, and comparing 4 compounding models on the rel-f1 Formula 1 dataset.

Diagnostic: chain 11 (races enrich constructor_standings -> constructors), probe: MLP, task: ss_constructors_nationality
examples.output: super-multiplicative: measured=1.3271, predicted=0.6571, deviation=0.6700. Super-multiplicative: information from 2nd hop synergizes with 1st hop, suggesting conditional dependence (information from hop1 helps decode hop2).

examples.input: Diagnostic: chain 12 (races enrich constructor_results -> constructors), probe: MLP, task: ss_constructors_nationality
examples.output: sub-multiplicative: measured=1.2052, predicted=1.4879, deviation=0.2827. Sub-multiplicative: information loss in aggregation chain, possibly due to high fanout variance or feature overlap.

examples.input: Diagnostic: chain 9 (constructors enrich results -> drivers), probe: MLP, task: driver-top3
examples.output: sub-multiplicative: measured=1.2873, predicted=1.3807, deviation=0.0933. Sub-multiplicative: information loss in aggregation chain, possibly due to high fanout variance or feature overlap.

examples.input: Cross-dataset comparison: rel-f1 vs rel-stack (prior work)
Definitive conclusion on JRN multiplicative compounding for rel-f1
examples.output: Compounding holds on rel-f1. Best compounding model: log_linear. Probe type matters. MLP R2=-4.196, GBM R2=-6.961. Both dataset-driven and probe-type-driven differences observed.
examples.predict_our_method: Compounding holds on rel-f1. Best compounding model: log_linear. Probe type matters. MLP R2=-4.196, GBM R2=-6.961. Both dataset-driven and probe-type-driven differences observed.
